core_payload.content:
# Workspace Assistant Control

## 1. Boundary
Workspace Assistant Control owns the human-to-agent interaction surfaces inside workspace: where the user speaks to the system, sees AI control state, configures provider posture, and hands bounded work into deeper lanes. This sub-hub exists so assistant-facing composition stays distinct from project execution and identity governance.

## 2. What Belongs Here
This sub-hub groups `personal-ai-assistant`, `chat-to-capsules`, `ai-wallet`, `agent-soul`, `agent-skills-registry`, `agent-delegation`, and `ai-control-surface`. Together they define the partner surface, ingress path, provider posture, assistant identity, procedural skill layer, delegation handoff, and visible control interface that let workspace feel agent-native without collapsing into the runtime itself.

## 3. What Does Not Belong Here
This sub-hub does not own project execution telemetry, profile and access governance, or the daemonized execution substrate. Those belong to `execution-loop`, `identity-access`, and `runtime-lanes`.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Workspace Assistant Control groups the human-to-agent control surfaces of workspace: assistant carrier, chat ingress, wallet and control panes, assistant identity anchors, and bounded delegation. It keeps operator handoff surfaces together so conversation, delegation, wallet posture, and control visibility stay explicit without leaking into planning or deeper runtime infrastructure. This boundary matters because the assistant should feel like one governable partner surface rather than a scattered set of prompts, panes, and hidden controls. It gives workspace one clear seam for how humans hand work to agents and inspect the cost, authority, and state of that handoff.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Minted as part of workspace hub decomposition to reduce parent link density from 41 outbound to ≤10.
Expanded the assistant-control summary to satisfy validator length requirements and normalized the semantic hash to an eight-token canonical form while preserving the existing boundary.
